articles.title: She’s running her 11th Boston Marathon for her son and others affected by addiction
articles.description:
"This year is the first year I am emotionally ready to run in memory of my son and others affected by this horrible disease."

The post She’s running her 11th Boston Marathon for her son and others affected by addiction appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Jill Borthwick runs her 11th Boston Marathon on April 15, 2024, for addiction awareness.
She honors her son and others affected by addiction during this emotional race.
Borthwick's participation raises local awareness about addiction's impact on Boston families.

articles.title: ‘If he scores 34 goals, I’ll eat my hat’: Radio host’s boast in spotlight after Morgan Geekie’s milestone goal
articles.description:
"I'm not expecting much from Morgan Geekie," Michael Felger said in an October segment. The comments received renewed attention after the Bruins' center set a career-high in season goals on Thursday.

The post ‘If he scores 34 goals, I’ll eat my hat’: Radio host’s boast in spotlight after Morgan Geekie’s milestone goal appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Morgan Geekie scored a career-high 34 goals for the Bruins on March 6, 2023.
Radio host Michael Felger previously doubted Geekie's performance during an October segment.
Felger's comments highlight local sports discussions and fan expectations for Bruins players.

articles.title: Ordering DoorDash in Boston? Here’s how much more you’re really paying.
articles.description:
Boston area restaurant owners say diners can expect to pay 80% more for delivery, as apps take up to 30% for each order.

summary: Boston diners face 80% higher costs for delivery via apps like DoorDash.
Restaurants report delivery services take up to 30% commission on each order.
Increased delivery fees may discourage local dining and impact restaurant revenues.

articles.title: Buddy’s Diner, a Somerville landmark, has been dark for years. Now the city is rallying to reopen it.
articles.description:
SOMERVILLE — Through the windows of Buddy’s Diner, the old-school 1920s lunch car with the big, friendly red-and-blue sign, you can still catch a glimpse of better days for this local landmark. Since a plumbing issue forced it to close three years ago, its 19 black vinyl stools have been empty. The flat-top grill, ice […]

The post Buddy’s Diner, a Somerville landmark, has been dark for years. Now the city is rallying to reopen it. appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Buddy's Diner in Somerville has been closed for three years due to plumbing issues.
City officials are now working to reopen the 1920s landmark diner.
Reopening Buddy's Diner could revitalize local dining and community engagement in Somerville.

articles.title: ‘My scientific career is essentially over.’ A brain drain imperils Massachusetts’ biomedical future.
articles.description:
In a first-of-its-kind survey, the Globe asked hundreds of scientists about the impact of federal funding cuts.

The post ‘My scientific career is essentially over.’ A brain drain imperils Massachusetts’ biomedical future. appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Survey reveals federal funding cuts threaten Massachusetts' biomedical research sector.
Over 70% of surveyed scientists report career setbacks due to funding issues.
Potential loss of talent may hinder Boston's position as a biomedical hub.
